mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer spending that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days as demand momentum gets priced in.
failure_condition: Positive directional accuracy falls below 52% over any 3-year rolling window or the Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack triggers downward earnings revisions and reduced consumer spending forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated consumer price inflation and federal funds rate create a stagflationary discount rate shock that compresses equity risk premiums and profit margins simultaneously, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces a Rank IC magnitude below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-recession recoveries signals capacity normalization and operating leverage expansion, producing strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days only in early-cycle environments.
failure_condition: During post-recession recoveries, INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with greater than 58% directional accuracy over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts sustained above 2000 thousand units indicate durable construction activity and household formation that lift cyclical sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered periods produce positive 60-day directional accuracy below 55% or mean forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment growth alongside a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightness is translating into aggregate income growth without supply-side friction, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and UNRATE produces directional accuracy below 55% or a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index growth erodes real corporate earnings and raises expectations of future monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days as discount rates reprice higher.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward returns remains above zero over any 2-year rolling window, or negative directional accuracy falls below 53%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth in late-cycle expansions reflects sustained aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth before margin compression sets in, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with greater than 55% directional accuracy, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).
